title: Thievery Preparation
content: Before running operations, gather current intel and evaluate target defense, thieves, and kingdom situation.

title: Thief Density
content: Thief strength affects operation success. Maintain enough thieves for intelligence and sabotage actions.

title: Intel First Rule
content: Always gather intelligence before committing attacks or operations. Old information can lead to poor decisions.

title: Spy on Throne Workflow
content: Use Spy on Throne first to understand the target province before deciding whether additional operations or attacks are worthwhile.

title: Spy on Military Workflow
content: Use Spy on Military to evaluate enemy troop numbers and determine attack feasibility.

title: Survey Workflow
content: Use Survey to identify building layouts and find weaknesses in enemy province design.

title: Nightstrike Priority
content: Nightstrike is most valuable against provinces with high offensive military that threaten kingdom attacks.

title: Wizard Kill Priority
content: Assassinating wizards is valuable against provinces relying heavily on magic.

title: Population Pressure
content: Reducing enemy peasants weakens economy, income, and recovery speed.

title: Economic Sabotage
content: Economic operations reduce enemy resources and limit their ability to rebuild.

title: Honor Warfare
content: Honor manipulation can weaken enemy bonuses and affect province effectiveness.

title: Operation Selection
content: Choose operations based on the target role. Attackers, mages, and thieves have different vulnerabilities.

title: Thief Defense
content: Protect thieves because losing thief strength reduces intelligence and sabotage capability.

title: Intel Chain
content: A complete intel chain uses multiple information sources to build an accurate target picture.

title: Pre Attack Checklist
content: Before attacking verify target intel, offense requirements, defense values, and kingdom coordination.
